title: Objects and classes converted or coerced to strings should define a toString() method
description: Objects and classes converted or coerced to strings should define a "toString()" methodAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode Smellstringobjecttype-dependentWhen callingtoString()or coercing into a string an object that doesn’t implement its owntoStringmethod, it returns[object Object]which is often not what was intended.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen using an object in a string context, a developer wants to get the string representation of the state of an object, so obtaining[object Object]is probably not the intended behaviour and might even denote a bug.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since10.1Analyze
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Consider defining custom toString() method to avoid [object Object] output
